meaning: adj.冒泡的,沸腾的,热情的,热情洋溢的
examples: The Prime Minister was in ebullient mood.
John was as bouncy and ebullient as Helen was diffident and reserved.
She looked tired and much less ebullient than when I saw her in jail.
When he took his seat at Westminster in 1972, he arrived in London in ebullient fashion.
But apart from a few cuts and bruises, he seems back to his normal, ebullient self.
